message:
test: add ~32 edge-case tests across error and ondisk-btrfs crates

Hardens two crates with targeted edge-case coverage:
- ffs-error: 10 tests (Send+Sync check, IoError ErrorKind→EIO fallthrough,
  MvccConflict zero values, Unicode payloads, raw errno precedence, ? chaining)
- ffs-ondisk btrfs: 22 tests (parse_from_image, BtrfsKey/BtrfsPhysicalMapping
  equality, header min/max sizes, sys_chunk_array truncation, logical-to-physical
  boundary/multi-chunk mapping, zero sectorsize/nodesize, zero stripesize allowed,
  superblock Debug/Clone, leaf zero items, KeyPtr traits)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1j8z","title":"S3-FIFO invariant panic hardening in ffs-block","description":"Replace panic-on-drift behavior in S3-FIFO hit/miss/rebalance paths with deterministic self-healing so transient queue-map inconsistencies do not abort process. Keep capacity and correctness invariants while preserving perf gates.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-22T06:19:17.265664366Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T06:25:00.503790453Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T06:25:00.503767510Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["block","s3fifo","stability"],"comments":[{"id":278,"issue_id":"bd-1j8z","author":"SunnyElm","text":"Implemented S3-FIFO invariant panic hardening in crates/ffs-block/src/lib.rs. Replaced panic-on-drift behavior in S3 hit/miss and overflow handling with deterministic recovery: (1) hit-path repairs stale ghost/missing loc metadata back into resident queues, (2) miss-path drops stale resident metadata instead of triggering debug-assert panic path, (3) overflow path runs bounded clean-eviction recovery and overflow-only queue reconciliation, then logs invariant violation without aborting process. Added reconciliation guard for pending admissions to preserve miss-before-insert lifecycle and avoid false drops. Validation: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --workspace --all-targets; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test -p ffs-block --all-features --lib; cargo test -p ffs-block --all-features --test perf_regression_e2e -- --nocapture; cargo test --workspace --all-features all pass. UBS scan on changed file now reports 2 critical (down from 5): remaining panics are dirty-eviction guard rails.","created_at":"2026-02-22T06:24:52Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1mdk","title":"EPIC: Bw-Trees for Extent Indexes (Graveyard Entry 8.7)","description":"# EPIC: Bw-Trees for Extent Indexes (Graveyard Entry 8.7)

## PURPOSE
Replace traditional B-tree extent indexes with Bw-Trees (Buzzword Trees) — lock-free delta-chain trees that allow concurrent readers and writers without any reader blocking. This is a long-term performance optimization for the extent index hot path.

## BACKGROUND
From the Alien CS Graveyard entry 8.7:
- Bw-Tree (Levandoski et al., ICDE 2013): latch-free B-tree with delta chains
- Key insight: instead of in-place mutation, append delta records to page
- Readers never block: they traverse the delta chain from newest to oldest
- Writers use CAS to append deltas (no locks)
- Periodic consolidation merges delta chains back into base pages

## DESIGN

### Architecture
- Mapping table: PageId -> AtomicPtr<PageDelta>
- Delta chain: linked list of delta records (insert, delete, split, merge)
- Base page: consolidated B-tree node
- Consolidation: background thread merges delta chains when they exceed threshold

### Delta Records
```rust
enum PageDelta {
    Insert { key: ExtentKey, value: ExtentValue, next: *const PageDelta },
    Delete { key: ExtentKey, next: *const PageDelta },
    Split { split_key: ExtentKey, new_page_id: PageId, next: *const PageDelta },
    Merge { removed_page_id: PageId, next: *const PageDelta },
    Base(BTreeNode),
}
```

### Concurrency
- Lookup: traverse mapping table -> delta chain -> base page (all read-only, no locks)
- Insert/Delete: create delta, CAS into mapping table (retry on conflict)
- Split/Merge: structural modification via delta + CAS
- Consolidation: create new base page, CAS to replace delta chain

### Extent Index Integration
- Replace extent B-tree in ffs-ext4 and ffs-btrfs with Bw-Tree
- Same lookup API: find_extent(inode, logical_block) -> PhysicalExtent
- Same mutation API: insert_extent, delete_extent, split_extent

## CURRENT PRIORITY
P2 — performance optimization, not blocking correctness. Current B-tree works correctly.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Lock-free lookup: readers never block on writers
- [ ] CAS-based insert/delete: no mutex on hot path
- [ ] Delta chain consolidation prevents unbounded chain growth
- [ ] Benchmark: lookup throughput under concurrent writers vs B-tree with locks
- [ ] Correctness: all B-tree invariants maintained (sorted, balanced, split/merge correct)
- [ ] Drop-in replacement for current extent index

## Success Criteria
1. All children closed
2. E2E benchmark (bd-1mdk.4) shows improvement over locked B-tree
3. Unit tests (bd-1mdk.3) pass
4. Delta chain consolidation compacts correctly
5. Mapping table is lock-free (no mutex in hot path)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:25:46.207227957Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T00:20:07.541669980Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T00:20:07.541602964Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bwtree","index","lockfree"],"comments":[{"id":98,"issue_id":"bd-1mdk","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SECOND-PASS REVIEW (lint fix): Epic is missing ## Success Criteria section. Add measurable success criteria before any children are marked closed. Example format: '## Success Criteria\n1. All children closed\n2. E2E test suite passes\n3. Feature parity gate green'","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:05:41Z"},{"id":171,"issue_id":"bd-1mdk","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Cluster: concurrency_perf
Mapped graveyard sections: 14.10 EBR + 15.1 S3-FIFO + 15.4 Parallel WAL
EV score: 13.333333333333334 (Impact*Confidence*Reuse / (Effort*Friction))
Priority tier: S/A
Budgeted mode: max_chain_len=64, wal_flush_batch=256, dirty_ratio_cap=0.8
Fallback trigger: single-writer conservative mode with bounded queue

Required proof artifacts for execution on this bead:
- Baseline + hotspot evidence before changes.
- Isomorphism proof block per lever.
- Evidence-ledger event for decision path + fallback invocation.
- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:26Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2lpe","title":"Implement zero-copy I/O paths where possible","description":"# Implement zero-copy I/O paths where possible

## GOAL
Minimize data copies on I/O paths, applying mechanical sympathy principles.

## BACKGROUND
FUSE fundamentally requires kernel/userspace copies. But we can minimize userspace copies:
1. Direct buffer handoff between cache and FUSE
2. Vectored I/O for multi-block operations
3. Page-aligned allocations for OS optimization

## TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. BlockBuf Refcounting
```rust
pub struct BlockBuf {
    data: Arc<AlignedVec>,  // Shared, aligned buffer
}

impl BlockBuf {
    pub fn as_slice(&self) -> &[u8];
    pub fn clone_ref(&self) -> BlockBuf;  // Arc::clone
    pub fn make_mut(&mut self) -> &mut [u8]; // COW if shared
}
```

### 2. Aligned Allocation
```rust
pub struct AlignedVec {
    ptr: NonNull<u8>,
    len: usize,
    cap: usize,
}

impl AlignedVec {
    pub fn new(size: usize, align: usize) -> Self;
    // Align to page size (4096) for direct I/O compatibility
}
```

### 3. Vectored I/O
```rust
pub trait VectoredBlockDevice: BlockDevice {
    fn read_vectored(
        &self,
        blocks: &[BlockNumber],
        bufs: &mut [BlockBuf],
        cx: &Cx,
    ) -> Result<()>;
    
    fn write_vectored(
        &self,
        blocks: &[BlockNumber],
        bufs: &[BlockBuf],
        cx: &Cx,
    ) -> Result<()>;
}
```

### 4. FUSE Buffer Handoff
- Use fuser reply buffer directly where possible
- Avoid intermediate Vec allocations
- Consider mmap for large reads (if FUSE supports)

## TESTS
1. Unit: BlockBuf refcounting semantics
2. Unit: Aligned allocation alignment
3. Integration: Vectored I/O correctness
4. Benchmark: Copy count measurement

## LOGGING
- Buffer allocation (trace): size, alignment
- Copy detected (trace): source, dest, size
- Vectored I/O (trace): block count

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. No unnecessary copies in hot paths
2. Memory usage not increased
3. Performance improvement measurable
4. Correctness preserved","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:55:26.339499830Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T15:21:53.291811560Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T15:21:53.291782436Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","optimization","perf"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2lpe","depends_on_id":"bd-3ib","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:57:00.318748579Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2lpe","depends_on_id":"bd-3ib.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:53:29.832494790Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":206,"issue_id":"bd-2lpe","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:32Z"}]}
